pattern: Three reversals — the whole array, then the first k elements, then the remaining — reposition every element in O(n) time with O(1) extra space.
hints: Rotating right by k is the same as rotating right by k mod n; handle that reduction first.
Think about what a reversal does to a sub-segment and how two reversals of complementary sub-segments together produce a rotation.
Reverse the entire array, then reverse the first k elements, then reverse elements from index k to the end.

edge_inputs.description: standard example, rotate 7-element array right by 3
k equals array length, array must remain unchanged
k larger than array length, effective rotation is k mod n
single element, any k leaves array unchanged

stress.description: large array with k potentially larger than n
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(-(2**31), 2**31) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(0, n * 2)
    return [nums, k]
